reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 6.3% and 20-day momentum is 18.4%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 7.0% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 2.6%, downside volatility near 1.9%, and a recent max drawdown of 6.9%.
Volume confirmation is -30.0% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 3.4% versus SPY over 5 days and 5.3% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.85, confidence 0.75, and 0.5 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.85, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 2 articles across 2 sources, 0.5 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.64, average source quality 0.78, confidence 0.75.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC -0.02).
Overall model score is 0.217 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Market Analysis: Tesla And Competitors In Automobiles Industry - Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA)
news_evidence.llm_reason: Direct analysis of Tesla and competitors with balance-sheet and ratio commentary. Informative for investor sentiment and relative valuation but appears to be analytical/background content rather than new, material company-specific news—limited immediate impact on near-term price.

news_evidence.title: The Gold Update: Gold Turns Higher While S&P Warning Signs Flash
news_evidence.llm_reason: Macro market commentary (gold, S&P warning signs). Could influence broad risk sentiment that affects TSLA, but it is not Tesla-specific and unlikely to drive company-level moves without a larger market selloff or confirming macro data.
